Ensure a Linux environment with at least 6 GPUs (e.g., 6x NVIDIA 3070 Ti) and proper CUDA setup
Clone the HuggingFace transformers repository: `git clone https://github.com/huggingface/transformers.git`
Navigate into the cloned transformers directory: `cd transformers`
Install transformers in editable mode with dependencies: `pip install -e .`
Create a new directory outside transformers, e.g., `mkdir ~/test && cd ~/test`
Install DeepSpeed and any other required dependencies: `pip install deepspeed`
Create a new shell script file named `test_tune.sh` in the test directory
Copy the exact content of `training/autotuning/hf/gpt2-xl/test_tune.sh` from the DeepSpeedExamples repository into `test_tune.sh`
Modify inside `test_tune.sh` the `HF_PATH` variable to point to the local transformers path, e.g., `HF_PATH=../transformers`
Set `NGPUS=6` inside `test_tune.sh` to match available GPUs
Ensure the script `test_tune.sh` is executable: `chmod +x test_tune.sh`
Run the script with tuning mode: `./test_tune.sh tune`
Observe the output logs and verify that it reaches the autotuning phase with logs similar to `[Start] Running autotuning`
Check for the error message in the logs: `ParquetConfig.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'token'`
Validate that the error occurs during the model info profiling phase, typically after scheduler writes config files and tries to launch experiments
Confirm that the error is reproducible on multiple runs with the same setup and configuration
Optionally, capture the full error traceback and log output to validate the exact exception origin and message
Assert that no valid DeepSpeed autotuning configuration is found and the script ends with a message indicating no optimal config
Document the PyPI package versions of transformers and deepspeed installed to correlate with the bug reproducibility
